choice: Give each Security/Holding a **kind**: (a) *marketable security* — has a ticker, uses its own historic series + beta; (b) *asset-class proxy* — no ticker, uses a class series + class beta (the fallback already named for thin-history assets); (c) *non-market fixed* — cash/stable-value, beta≈0, modeled at a configured nominal or inflation-linked rate. Real estate and pensions/annuities: model real estate as an asset-class-proxy (or held-flat) line in net worth but confirm it is **not drawn down** by the withdrawal engine in v1, and treat a defined-benefit pension/annuity as an **income stream** (generic stream with COLA enum), not a Holding.
rationale: The beta-to-S&P generative process is undefined for a checking account or a house, and decision 62a1f0fb already established an asset-class-proxy fallback for assets lacking history — generalizing that into an explicit Holding 'kind' keeps the engine total-coverage without special cases. Routing pensions/annuities to the existing income-stream machinery (701e81b4) avoids inventing a second cashflow path. Real estate as a non-depleting net-worth line keeps it visible in actuals-vs-Plan without forcing illiquid-asset sale logic, which is heavier than v1 needs.
